steps.expected: Write 5 ShapedGlyph structs via SceneWriter, read back via SceneReader — all fields preserved
steps.observed: Test passed: glyph_id, x_advance, x_offset, y_offset all round-tripped correctly

evidence.detail: Test writes 5 ShapedGlyph entries with distinct glyph_id/advance/offset values via push_shaped_glyphs → push_text_runs → SceneWriter. Reads back via SceneReader.shaped_glyphs() and verifies field-by-field equality.

steps.observed: Test passed: all 5 nodes with all glyph fields preserved exactly

steps.expected: Raw bytes of ShapedGlyph arrays are identical after round-trip
steps.observed: Test passed: byte-exact equality confirmed

evidence.detail: First test writes 5+ text nodes with varying glyph counts (1,3,7,12,20) and reads all back asserting field-by-field equality. Second test compares raw bytes for exact binary round-trip.

assertions.title: Data buffer capacity for shaped text

steps.expected: 2100 ShapedGlyph entries across 21 runs fit within 64 KiB data buffer
steps.observed: Test passed: all data pushed without overflow, data_used <= DATA_BUFFER_SIZE

evidence.detail: Test allocates 2100 glyphs (21 runs of 100 each, 8 bytes per glyph = 16800 bytes total) and verifies push_shaped_glyphs succeeds for all runs and data_used <= DATA_BUFFER_SIZE (64 KiB)

assertions.title: Glyph ID data integrity across scene graph

steps.expected: Boundary glyph IDs (0, 1, 65534) written via SceneWriter read back identically via SceneReader
steps.observed: Test passed: glyph_id=0 (x_advance=100), glyph_id=1 (x_offset=-5, y_offset=3), glyph_id=65534 (x_advance=300, x_offset=10, y_offset=-10) all preserved

steps.expected: Second test with slightly different offset values also round-trips
steps.observed: Test passed: all boundary values preserved exactly

evidence.detail: Both tests write ShapedGlyph arrays with boundary glyph IDs (0, 1, 65534) through SceneWriter and read back via SceneReader, asserting exact equality on all fields including offsets

assertions.title: No regression in monospace text rendering

steps.expected: Shaping 'iiiWWW' with Source Code Pro produces 6 glyphs with identical x_advance
steps.observed: Test passed: all 6 glyphs have identical x_advance

evidence.detail: Test shapes 'iiiWWW' with SOURCE_CODE_PRO font, asserts len==6, then checks every glyph's x_advance equals the first glyph's x_advance

summary: Tested 15 assertions: 15 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. All unit tests for text shaping (VAL-SHAPE-001 through VAL-SHAPE-004), rasterization (VAL-RASTER-001, VAL-RASTER-002), glyph cache (VAL-CACHE-001 through VAL-CACHE-003), scene graph (VAL-SCENE-001 through VAL-SCENE-004), cross-area glyph ID integrity (VAL-CROSS-005), and monospace regression (VAL-E2E-004) pass successfully.
